Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 084 [Šurpu 4][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

⸢EN₂⸣ e-peš ri-is-bi [...]

(o 1) Incantation: [It is with you, Marduk], to heal and keep safe the committing of a beating [...], (you) who defeated the Asakku-demon.

o 22

bul-lu-ṭu šul-lu-mu ⸢d⸣[AMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

o 33

ša₂ ki-šit-te A₂.ZAG₂ NIG₂.GIG DINGIR-[šu ... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 3) [...] the taboo of one's god, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 44

an-zil-lu kub-bu-su pa-ša₂-[ru BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 4) To infringe a taboo (and) to release (it), [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 55

a-na al-ti ib-ri-šu a-la-ku pu-[uz-zu-ru BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 5) To go secretly to the wife of one's friend, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 66

a-na DUMU.MUNUS DINGIR-šu la₃ ZUu a-la-⸢ku⸣ [BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 6) To go unwittingly to the daughter of one's god, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 77

ep-qu mi-iḫ-ru ta-zi-⸢im⸣-[tu₂ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 7) Leprosy, adversity, complaint, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 88

⸢ti⸣-ri-iṣ ŠU.SI mi-ḫir-ti DINGIR-[MEŠ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 8) The pointing of the finger, complaint against the gods, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 99

⸢ki⸣-i na-ḫi-iš qa-bu-u la šu-[šu-ru BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 9) To say "how thriving," but not to make successful, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 1010

⸢DINGIR⸣ u₃ diš-tar kam!(NAGAR-)lu a-⸢na⸣ [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 10) [...] the irate god and goddess [...] to [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 1111

[DINGIR] šab-su diš-tar ze-ni-tu₂ [sul]-⸢lu-mu⸣ [BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 11) To reconcile the displeased [god], the angry goddess, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 1212

ki-⸢ṣir⸣ lib₃-bi DINGIR-MEŠ u diš-⸢tar⸣ [pa-ṭa]-⸢ru?⸣ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 12) To release the god's and goddess's anger, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 1313

ar₂-nu ⸢šu-su⸣-ḫu gil₂-⸢la⸣-[tu₂ šu-us]-⸢su⸣-u BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 13) To remove the transgression, to eradicate the sin, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 1414

ḫi-ṭi-tu₂ ⸢šul-lu⸣-mu lu₂⸢GIG⸣ [bu-lu]-⸢ṭu⸣ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 14) To correct the mistake, to heal the sick, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 1515

ma-aq-tu₂ šu-⸢ut⸣-[bu]-⸢u ŠU-MIN en⸣-[ši ...] BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 15) To make the fallen one stand up, [...] the weak one by the hand, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 1616

⸢šim⸣-ti ḪUL[ti₃ šu-pe]-⸢lu⸣ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 16) To overturn an evil fate, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 1717

⸢MAŠ₂⸣.GE₆ ḪUL[ti₃ du-um-mu]-⸢qu⸣ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 17) To improve an evil dream, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 1818

⸢d⸣LAMMA SIG₅ ana x x [...] BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 18) [...] a good tutelary goddess upon ... [...], ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 1919

[...] MAŠ₂.GE₆-⸢MEŠ⸣ A₂-MEŠ ⸢GISKIM⸣-[MEŠ ... la₃] ⸢TE⸣e BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 19) To avert [...] of dreams, ominous conditions (and) signs, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 2020

[ma]-⸢mit uk-ku⸣-šu ⸢munusPEŠ₄⸣ qa₂-⸢du⸣ [... šul]-⸢lu⸣-mu ⸢BU⸣<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 20) To expel a curse, to keep the pregant woman together with [...] safe, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 2121

[šu]-⸢lu⸣-ud-du šu-mu šur-[šu-u] BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 21) To cause to give birth (and) to provide a son, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 2222

[...] ⸢ša₂-su⸣-u ⸢EN⸣ [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 22) To shout [..., ...] the lord, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 2323

[ra-še]-⸢e re-e-ma?⸣ x [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 23) To have mercy, [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 2424

[...] x kun-⸢nu⸣-u MU DINGIR-[MEŠ ... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 24) To treat kindly [...] ..., [...] the name of the gods, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 2525

[giš]⸢MA₂⸣ ma-⸢li⸣-tu [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 25) [...] a full boat [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 2626

[ḫa-sa]-su [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 26) To remember [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

(14 lines missing)
o 4141

ša₂ ⸢GIDIM⸣ [lem-nu iṣ-ba-tu-šu BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 41) Whom an [evil] ghost [has seized, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 4242

ša₂ A.LA₂ [lem-nu iṣ-ba-tu-šu BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 42) Whom an [evil] alû-demon [has seized, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 4343

ša₂ UDUG [lem-nu iṣ-ba-tu-šu BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 43) Whom an [evil] utukku-demon [has seized, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 4444

ša₂ GAL₅.LA₂ lem-nu iṣ-ba-tu-šu [BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 44) Whom an evil gallû-demon has seized, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 4545

ša₂ DINGIR lem-nu iṣ-⸢ba-tu⸣-šu [BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 45) Whom an evil god has seized, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 4646

ša₂ MAŠKIM₂ lem-nu ⸢iṣ⸣-[ba]-⸢tu⸣-šu [BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 46) Whom an evil rābiṣu-demon has seized, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 4747

ša₂ MAŠKIM₂ ki-šit-te DINGIR-šu₂ [iṣ-ba]-⸢tu⸣-šu ⸢BU⸣<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 47) Whom a rābiṣu-demon defeated by his god has seized, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 4848

ša₂ dDIM₉.ME [iṣ]-⸢ba⸣-tu-šu ⸢BU⸣<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 48) Whom Lamaštu has seized, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 4949

ša₂ dDIM₉.ME.A [iṣ]-⸢ba⸣-tu-šu BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 49) Whom a labāṣu-demon has seized, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 5050

ša₂ dDIM₉.ME.LAGAB [iṣ]-ba-tu-šu BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 50) Whom an ahhāzu-demon has seized, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 5151

ša₂ mim-ma lem-nu ⸢iṣ⸣-ba-tu-šu BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 51) Whom something evil has seized, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 5252

ar-ni ma-mit ḫi-ṭi-tu₂ gil₂-la-tu₂ MU DINGIR šib-sat DINGIR-MEŠ <mi>-ḫir-te DINGIR ta-zi-im-tu₂ ma-mit

(o 52) Transgression, curse, mistake, sin, the name of the god, the anger of the gods, the complaint against the god, lament, oath, curse of father and mother, elder brother (and) elder sister, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 5353

ar-rat AD u AMA ŠEŠ GALu NIN GALtu₂ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

o 5454

it-ti-ka lip-šur-ru DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 54) May the great gods release (all these) together with you, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 5555

1en lip-šur dša₂-maš qu-ra-du BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 55) First, may Šamaš, the warrior, release, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 5656

2u₂ lip-šur d30 dNERGALₓ(U.GUR) BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 56) Second, may Sin (and) Nergal release, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 5757

3 lip-šur d15 d⸢PAP.SUKKAL⸣ da-nu-ni-tu₂ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 57) 3, may Ištar, Papsukkal (and) Annunitu release, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 5858

4 lip-šur da-nu₃ u dEN.LIL₂ de₂-a dNIN.MAḪ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 58) 4, may Anu and Ellil, Ea (and) Ninmah release, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 5959

5 lip-šur dIŠKUR dMAŠ dza-ba₄-ba₄ dTIŠPAK dNIN.GIR₂.SU BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 59) 5, may Adad, Ninurta, Zababa, Tišpak (and) Ningirsu release, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 6060

6 lip-šur dURAŠ dMES dASAR.RE dASAR.LU₂.ḪI AN.GAL dtu-tu BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(o 60) 6, may Uraš, Marduk, Asare, Asalluhi, Angal, (and) Tutu release, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 6161

7 lip-⸢šur d7⸣.BI DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ DINGIR-MEŠ šu-tu₂-ḫa-a il-tu₂ lip-ṭur BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma1

(o 61) 7, may the Seven, great gods, release; may the extended gods absolve the obligation, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

o 6262

ra-kis-tu₂ li-sap-pi-ḫu ki-ṣir lum!(IGI-)nu li-par-ri-ru ka-si-tu₂  ; li-⸢ra-mu⸣-[u BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 62) May they disperse the conspiracy; may they dissolve the knot of evil; may they release the magical constraint, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 6363

ma-mit lip-ṭu-ru niš DINGIR lip-su-su ar₂-nam li-is-⸢su⸣-[ḫu BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 63) May they absolve the curse; may they cancel (the oath on) the life of the god; may they remove the transgression, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 6464

⸢gil₂⸣-la-tu₂ li-is-su-u ḫi-ṭi-tu₂ li-šal-li-⸢mu⸣ [BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 64) May they eradicate the sin; may they correct the mistake, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

o 6565

⸢lu₂GIG⸣ lib-luṭ ma-aq-tu₂ lit-bi ⸢ka⸣-[su-u ... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(o 65) May the patient recover; may the fallen one stand up; [...] the captive [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

Reverse
r 1r 1

⸢ṣab⸣-tu li-in-ṭa-⸢šir⸣ ša₂ E₂ ṣi-bit-te [... BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(r 1) May the captive be released; [...] the prisoner [..., ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

r 22

ša₂ DINGIR-šu₂ diš₈-tar₂-šu₂ KI-šu₂ šab-su ina u₄-me an-ne₂-e ⸢li⸣-[is-li-mu KI-šu₂ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma]

(r 2) May his god (and) his goddess, who are angry with him, be reconciled [with him] on this day, [ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe)];

r 33

ki-ṣir lib₃-bi DINGIR-šu₂ u diš₈-⸢tar₂⸣-šu₂ ⸢ša₂ NENNI A NENNI li⸣-[paṭ]-⸢ṭir-aš₂-šu₂⸣ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 3) May his god's and his goddess's anger with so-and-so, son of so-and-so, be dispelled from him, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 44

še-ret-su lit-ta-bi-⸢ik⸣ ina u₄-me an-ni-⸢i⸣ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 4) May his guilt be expunged on this day, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 55

lu pa-ša₂-ra-ku lu pa-ṭa-ra-šu₂ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 5) May it be released from me; may it be released from him, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 66

ṭup-pi ar₂-ni-šu₂ ḫi-ṭa-ti-šu₂ gil₂-la-⸢te⸣-šu₂ ana A-MEŠ ŠUBu BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 6) May the tablets of his transgressions, his mistakes, (and) his sins be thrown into the water, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 77

ḫi-ṭa-tu-šu₂ li-ma-ša₂-a BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 7) May his mistakes be forgotten, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 88

gil₂-la-⸢tu-šu₂⸣ li-pa-si-sa BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 8) May his sins be cancelled, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 99

ma-ma-⸢tu-šu₂⸣ li-pa-šir BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 9) May his curses be undone, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 1010

GIG-MEŠ-[šu₂] li-tak-ki-šu₂ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 10) May his illnesses be expelled, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 1111

di-iʾ-a-šu₂ ⸢di⸣-lip-ta-šu₂ ni-sa-su la DU₁₀ub UZU-šu₂ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 11) His headache, his anxiety, his wailing (and) his poor physical health, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 1212

u₈-a-a la [ṣa]-lal-šu₂ na-zaq-šu₂ ni-sa-su ta-ne₂-eḫ-šu₂ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 12) (His) woe, his sleeplessness, his worrying, his wailing (and) his distress, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 1313

ina u₄-me an-ni-i ša₂ NENNI A NENNI uk-ki-ša₂ ina SU-šu₂ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 13) Expel (them) from the body of so-and-so, son of so-and-so, on this day, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 1414

a-na ḫi-is-sa-at MU-ku-nu el-li li-kiš li-⸢se⸣ li-dap-pir BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 14) At the mention of your pure names, may it be expelled, become distant, (and) move away, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe);

r 1515

ar-ni ma-mit ša₂ <ana šu>-un!(E-)zu-qu a-me-lu-tu₂ ša₂-⸢kin⸣ BU<(bul-lu-ṭu)> šul-lu-mu dAMAR.UTU it-ti-ka-ma

(r 15) The transgression (and) curse which are placed to upset mankind, ditto (it is with you, Marduk, to heal and keep safe).

r 1616

li-iz-zi-zu da-nunu₃ u an-tu₄ li-ne₂-eʾ-u mur!(MAR-)ṣu

(r 16) May Anu and Antu be present; may they ward off illness.

r 1717

KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz)> dEN.LIL₂ EN NIBRUki ina KA-šu₂ <ša₂> la BALu TIsu liq-bi

(r 17) Ellil, lord of Nippur, ditto (may he be present); may he decree his healing with his command that does not change.

r 1818

KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz)> de₂-a EN te-ne₂-še-ti ša₂ ŠU-MIN-šu₂ KU₃-MEŠ DU₃a NAM.LU₂.U₁₉.⸢LU⸣

(r 18) Ea, lord of humankind, whose pure hands created mankind, ditto (may he be present).

r 1919

KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz)> d30 EN ITI li-pa-šir ma-ma-te-šu₂

(r 19) Sin, lord of the month, ditto (may he be present); may he dispel his curses.

r 2020

KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz)> d⸢ša₂-maš EN⸣ [...] ⸢li-pa⸣-ṭir ar-ni

(r 20) Šamaš, lord of [...], ditto (may he be present); may he release the transgressions.

r 2121

[...] ⸢mur-ṣu⸣

(r 21) [...] illness.

r 2222

[...] x [...]

(r 22) [...] ... [...].

(11 lines missing)
r 3434

KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz)> dGU.LA a-⸢zu⸣-[gal-la-tu ...]

(r 34) Gula, chief physician, ditto (may she be present); [...].

r 3535

KI.MIN<(li-iz-ziz)> dba-ba₆ di-lip-⸢ta⸣-[šu₂ ...]

(r 35) Baba, ditto (may she be present); [... his] anxiety.

(catchline)
r 3636

⸢EGIR⸣-šu₂ EN₂ aš₂ ḫul gal₅-[la₂-gin₇ ...]

(r 36) After this: Incantation: A bad curse like a gallu-demon [...].

(colophon)
r 3737

ki-⸢i⸣ KA SUMUN-šu₂ ⸢SAR-ma⸣ ba₃-[ri₃]

(r 37) Written and checked in accordance with the wording of its original. Tablet of Aššur-šumu-iddina, scribal apprentice, son of [...]-šumu-ukin, scribe, [son of ...]-emuranni, scribe, antaṣû-scribe.

r 3838

[IM m]⸢d⸣aš+šur—šu-[mu?]—⸢MU⸣na lu₂ŠAB.TUR DUMU

r 3939

[m...]—MU—⸢DU⸣ [lu₂]⸢ṭup⸣-šar₃

r 4040

[DUMU m...—IGI].LA₂-an-ni lu₂⸢ṭup⸣-šar₃ an-ta-ṣu-u

r 4141

[ša] it-tab-ba-lu dša₂-maš IGI-⸢MIN⸣-šu₂ lit-bal-šu₂

(r 41) [Whoever] takes away (this tablet), may Šamaš take away his eyes.

r 4242

itiŠU U₄ 24-KAM!(NAGAR) lim-me mSILIMmu—⸢EN⸣—[la-aš₂-me]

(r 42) Duʾuzu (month IV), day 24, eponymate of Šulmu-bel-lašme, governor of Der, in the time of [...], great king, mighty king, king of the universe, king of [...].

r 4343

lu₂ša₂-kin₃ iride-e-ri ina LA₂iṣ [...]

r 4444

LUGAL GALu MAN dan-nu MAN ŠU₂ ⸢MAN⸣ [...]

1The reading of šu-tu₂-ha-a is uncertain.
